text: An AM wave has 1800 W of total power content. For 100% modulation the carrier should have power content equal to
explanation:
Total power of AM wave, $P_t = 1800$ W.
For 100% modulation, depth of modulation, $m = 1$.
If $P_c$ be the carrier power, then
$P_t = P_c \left(1 + \frac{m^2}{2}\right)$
$P_t = P_c \left(1 + \frac{1}{2}\right)$
$P_t = \frac{3}{2} P_c$
$P_c = \frac{2}{3} P_t = \frac{2}{3} \times 1800 = 1200$ W.
name: Questions Item 1

text: A speech signal of 3 kHz is used to modulate a carrier signal of frequency 1 MHz, using amplitude modulation. The frequencies of the side bands will be
explanation:
Frequency of carrier signal, $f_c = 1$ MHz.
Frequency of message (speech) signal, $f_m = 3$ kHz = 0.003 MHz.
Frequency of upper side band $= f_c + f_m = 1.003$ MHz.
Frequency of lower side band $= f_c - f_m = 0.997$ MHz.
name: Questions Item 2

text: A signal of frequency 10 kHz and peak voltage 10 V is used to amplitude modulate a carrier of frequency 1 MHz and peak voltage 20 V. The side-band frequencies in kHz are
explanation:
The side band frequencies are given as
$f = f_c \pm f_s$
$f = 1 \times 10^6 \pm 10 \times 10^3$ Hz = (1000 ± 10) × 10³ Hz.
So, the side band frequencies in kHz is 1010 kHz and 990 kHz.
name: Questions Item 3

text: A TV tower has a height of 5 m in a region of population density $\frac{1000}{\pi}$ per square kilometre. Number of people that can receive the transmission is nearly (in thousands)
explanation:
Maximum range of this transmission $d = \sqrt{2 h R_e}$.
Population covered, $P_c = n \times \pi d^2 = 1000 \times 2 h R_e = 1000 \times 2 \times 5 \times 10^{-3} \times 6400 = 64000$.
name: Questions Item 4

text: A transmitting antenna of height 20 m and the receiving antenna of height $h$ are separated by a distance of 40 km for satisfactory communication in line of sight (LOS) mode. Then the value of $h$ is (Radius of earth is 6400 km)
explanation:
Maximum distance of line of sight mode
$d_m = \sqrt{2 R h_t} + \sqrt{2 R h_r}$
$40 = 16 + \sqrt{2 \times 6.4 \times 10^3 \times h_r \times 10^{-3}}$
$h_r = 45$ m.
name: Questions Item 5

text: The frequency suitable for beyond-the-horizon communication using sky waves is
explanation: The frequency suitable for beyond-the-horizon communication using sky wave is 10 MHz ($10^7$ Hz).
name: Questions Item 6

text: A TV tower has a height 160 m. Its coverage range is nearly (Earth's radius, $R_e = 6400$ km)
explanation:
Coverage range of TV tower
$d = \sqrt{2 R h} = \sqrt{2 \times 6.4 \times 10^6 \times 160} = 45255$ m = 45.255 km.
name: Questions Item 7

text: Co-axial cable, a widely used wire medium for transmission of signals offers a bandwidth of approximately
explanation: Co-axial cable primarily used for transmission of radio, television signals, which are in range of 3 MHz to 3 GHz. So, the bandwidth $\Delta f \approx 3000$ MHz.
name: Questions Item 8

text: If $E_c$ and $E_m$ are peak values of carrier and modulating signals, respectively then for 100% modulation
explanation: Modulation index $\mu = \frac{E_m}{E_c}$. For 100% modulation, $\mu = 1 \Rightarrow E_m = E_c$.
name: Questions Item 9

text: If a message signal of frequency 10 kHz and peak voltage 12 V is used to modulate a carrier wave of frequency 1 MHz, the modulation index is 0.6. To make the modulation index 0.75, the carrier peak voltage should be
explanation:
$m_1 = \frac{12}{E_{c1}} = 0.6 \Rightarrow E_{c1} = 20$ V.
$m_2 = 0.75 \Rightarrow E_{c2} = \frac{12}{0.75} = 16$ V.
Decreased by 4 V, i.e. 20%.
name: Questions Item 10
